acceptanceChecks.description: Handoff recommends minimal kernel records for binding scopes, bindings, patterns, and type constraints across functional, OO, logic, macro, and dynamic languages.

items.prompt: Do a focused design pass for Frontier Lang kernel paradigm semantics. Inspect src/index.js, src/index.d.ts, README.md, test/smoke.mjs, and prior research handoffs. Do not edit source files. Produce a concise handoff under research/ covering binding scopes, bindings, patterns, and type constraints. Include expected validation rules, test cases, and how this supports importing languages like JS/TS, Rust, Python, Prolog, Lisp/Racket macros, and OO dispatch.

acceptanceChecks.description: Handoff recommends target-neutral records for evaluation models, memory locations, effect regions, and runtime assumptions.

items.prompt: Design the Frontier Lang kernel records needed for imperative/runtime semantics: evaluation model, memory locations, effect regions, control regions, resource/capability links, and runtime assumptions. Do not edit source. Anchor recommendations to current UniversalAstLayerRecord and proofSpec APIs and include validation/test ideas.

acceptanceChecks.description: Handoff maps logic, actor, stack/concatenative, dataflow, array/numeric, and object models to structural kernel records.

items.prompt: Map non-mainstream programming paradigms into Frontier Lang kernel records: Prolog/logic rules and unification, Erlang actors/mailboxes, Forth/Factor stack effects, APL/Fortran array/numeric kernels, dataflow clocks, and object/message dispatch. Do not edit source. Recommend minimal records and validation/test cases.

acceptanceChecks.description: Handoff recommends macro expansion, reflection boundary, and lowering record shapes with source preservation links.

items.prompt: Design kernel-level records for macro expansion, reflection/dynamic runtime boundaries, and lowering/projection records. Use prior IR/source-map research. Do not edit source. Explain how records should connect to native AST losses, source maps, proofSpec, and merge evidence.

acceptanceChecks.description: Handoff lists focused smoke/fuzz/type tests needed for a paradigm semantics layer without overfitting to one target language.

items.prompt: Review current kernel tests and propose concrete tests for a new paradigm semantics universal AST layer. Do not edit source. Include good fixtures, broken-reference cases, fuzz strategy, and public d.ts expectations.

acceptanceChecks.description: Handoff explains what compiler and swarm sidecar summaries should expose once kernel paradigm records exist.

items.prompt: Inspect compiler/swarm sidecar patterns from current package docs/source if present in workspace context. Do not edit source. Recommend concise summary fields for paradigm semantics records so merge admission can query language features, risks, and unsupported constructs.
